evals.prompt: O processo de onboarding de clientes está a ser seguido pela equipa? Quero uma auditoria.
evals.expected_output: A quality audit report for the client onboarding process: POP retrieved (or audit halted if none exists), POP vs. practice table, NCR log with 5-Whys, PDCA corrective actions, and kb_ingest of the report.
expectations: The skill calls kb_search for the onboarding POP before auditing — if none exists, it halts and requests the COO to document it first via ops:pop-builder.
The POP vs. practice table lists every POP step with Expected / Observed / Conforming columns populated from evidence.
Each non-conformity record includes a full 5-Whys root-cause analysis that reaches a systemic root cause (not stops at Why 1).
Every PDCA corrective action has a single named owner (a dept head) and a deadline consistent with severity (Minor ≤ 30d, Major ≤ 14d, Critical ≤ 48h).
The completed audit report is ingested via kb_ingest with source 'audits/YYYY-MM-DD-<process-slug>.md'.

evals.prompt: Audit the sales proposal process — I want to know if we're actually following what we documented.
evals.expected_output: A quality audit of the sales proposal process with scope defined, conformity table, NCR log with evidence citations, corrective actions, and routing to the CRO.
expectations: The skill scopes the audit explicitly before the POP vs. practice walk: process name, process owner (CRO), POP version, and any prior open NCRs.
Every finding in the non-conformity log cites a concrete artefact or observation — not assertions without evidence.
The audit report includes a 'Positive observations' section noting what the process does well.
The routing step names the CRO as process owner, summarises the verdict, and states the corrective actions requiring their sign-off.
The skill does not block ongoing work — work continues while corrections are planned.

evals.prompt: Verifica se o processo de gestão de conteúdo do nosso blog está a ser seguido. Parece que as publicações não têm revisão antes de sair.
evals.expected_output: A quality audit of the content management process, identifying the missing review step as a non-conformity, with a 5-Whys analysis, PDCA action for the CMO, and report ingested.
expectations: The skill calls kb_search for the content management POP — if absent, stops and requests COO to document it first.
The alleged missing review step is investigated with evidence (artefact, observation, or user report) before being logged as an NCR.
The NCR for the missing review step is classified with a severity (Minor/Major/Critical) and that classification is justified.
The corrective action is owned by the CMO (as the content process owner) with a realistic deadline.
The report is ingested with source 'audits/YYYY-MM-DD-content-management.md'.
